Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

notes: All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require the completion of specific preparatory courses and/or minimum GPA requirements. For detailed information about the screening requirements for this major, please refer to "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below. Additional preparatory coursework listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section are not required for admission, but are strongly recommended. Completion of these courses will help ensure timely progress toward degree completion. More information and a list of screening majors and non-screening majors may be found on the Admissions website.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.

Economics/Mathematics Combined B.A. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer students must complete the following three courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a grade of C (2.0) or better in each course, and a cumulative minimum GPA of 2.8 in all required screening courses , by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: ECON 1 – Introductory Microeconomics: Resource Allocation and Market Structure ECON 2 – Introductory Macroeconomics: Aggregate Economic Activity MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below. These courses are prerequisites for upper-division courses required for the major. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Additional Information for Transfer Prospective transfer students should prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses, and may additionally complete courses that satisfy UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as time allows. For information on incorporating general education requirements into your pre-transfer academic plan, consult with your community college counselor. Please visit the Economics website to learn more about this major.
